title: diplazium sarawakense
intro: Diplazium sarawakense is a species of fern in the family Athyriaceae, commonly known as the Sarawak shield fern. This plant is native to Sarawak, a state in East Malaysia, and is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial activities. The health benefits of Diplazium sarawakense include the treatment of fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues, as well as its use as an antiseptic and wound healer. Traditionally, the plant is used in herbal preparations such as infusions and decoctions, which are consumed to reap its medicinal benefits.
